items.headline: Futures Edge Higher Amid Nvidia Earnings and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed modest gains this morning as investors digested Nvidia's robust earnings report, while remaining cautious due to persistent inflation concerns highlighted in the latest Federal Reserve minutes and ongoing geopolitical developments in the Middle East. Tech stocks are once again in focus, but broader market sentiment remains mixed. (Not financial advice)

items.headline: Applied Digital Soars on AI Campus Deal
items.summary: Shares of Applied Digital ($APLD) surged by 8% after the company announced a 15-year lease agreement for its Polaris Forge 3 AI campus, valued at up to $18.2 billion.

items.headline: AEVEX Jumps on Air Force Contracts and Strong Q1
items.summary: AEVEX ($AVX) gained 6% after securing $15.6 million in U.S. Air Force contracts and reporting stronger-than-expected first-quarter results.

items.headline: e.l.f. Beauty Up on Strong Q4 Performance
items.summary: e.l.f. Beauty ($ELF) rose 6% after delivering a solid beat on both top and bottom lines in its fourth-quarter earnings report.

items.headline: Intuit Tumbles on Workforce Reduction Plans
items.summary: Intuit ($INTU) shares fell 11% in after-hours trading after the company announced a 17% workforce reduction, despite beating third-quarter expectations.

items.headline: Target Drops Despite Earnings Beat, Cites Conservative Outlook
items.summary: Target ($TGT) shares declined nearly 4% following its first-quarter 2027 results, as conservative full-year guidance and ongoing cost headwinds offset an earnings per share beat.

items.headline: Fed Minutes Signal Potential for Rate Hikes Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Minutes from the April 28-29 Federal Reserve meeting revealed that a majority of policymakers are open to further rate hikes if inflation remains persistently above the 2% target. Consumer inflation is currently at 3.8% and wholesale prices at 6%.

items.headline: Nvidia Delivers Strong Q1, Announces Buyback and Dividend Hike
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) reported strong first-quarter fiscal 2027 results, surpassing Wall Street estimates. The company also announced an $80 billion share buyback program and increased its quarterly dividend to $0.25 per share.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions and Oil Prices Remain a Market Factor
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly concerning the U.S.-Iran situation, continue to influence market sentiment and contribute to rising oil prices. Hopes for a peace deal, however, have provided some intermittent relief.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads in Asia, Mixed Performance in US
items.summary: Asian markets saw a rally in technology shares, with South Korea's Kospi soaring 8.4% due to strong buying in tech giants like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. In the U.S., while tech stocks led a rally on Wednesday, pre-market signals suggest mixed performance with continued focus on AI-driven growth.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Expected Today
items.summary: Today's economic calendar includes the release of Global S&P PMIs Flash for May, Japanese Trade Balance for April, and U.S. Initial Jobless Claims for the week ending May 16.
